From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Mario Kart Live](/w/index.php?title=Mario_Kart_Live&redirect=no "Mario Kart Live"))

2020 video game

2020 video game

| Mario Kart Live: Home Circuit | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| [Developer](/wiki/Video_game_developer "Video game developer") | [Velan Studios](/wiki/Velan_Studios "Velan Studios") |
| [Publisher](/wiki/Video_game_publisher "Video game publisher") | [Nintendo](/wiki/Nintendo "Nintendo") |
| [Director](/wiki/Video_game_creative_director "Video game creative director") | Dan Doptis |
| [Producers](/wiki/Video_game_producer "Video game producer") | Yosuke Tamori Hiroshi Matsunaga Toyokazu Nonaka |
| [Programmer](/wiki/Video_game_programmer "Video game programmer") | Jan-Erik Steel |
| [Composer](/wiki/Video_game_composer "Video game composer") | Ryo Nagamatsu[[1]](#cite_note-1) |
| Series | *[Mario Kart](/wiki/Mario_Kart "Mario Kart")* |
| [Platform](/wiki/Computing_platform "Computing platform") | [Nintendo Switch](/wiki/Nintendo_Switch "Nintendo Switch") |
| Release | * WW: October 16, 2020 |
| [Genres](/wiki/Video_game_genre "Video game genre") | [Kart racing](/wiki/Kart_racing_game "Kart racing game"), [augmented reality](/wiki/Augmented_reality "Augmented reality") |
| Modes | [Single-player](/wiki/Single-player_video_game "Single-player video game"), [multiplayer](/wiki/Multiplayer_video_game "Multiplayer video game") |

***Mario Kart Live: Home Circuit*** is a 2020 [mixed reality](/wiki/Mixed_reality "Mixed reality") [kart racing game](/wiki/Kart_racing_game "Kart racing game") developed by [Velan Studios](/wiki/Velan_Studios "Velan Studios") and published by [Nintendo](/wiki/Nintendo "Nintendo") for the [Nintendo Switch](/wiki/Nintendo_Switch "Nintendo Switch"). It is the tenth game in the *[Mario Kart](/wiki/Mario_Kart "Mario Kart")* series, announced in celebration of the [35th anniversary](/wiki/Super_Mario_Bros._35th_Anniversary "Super Mario Bros. 35th Anniversary") of the *[Super Mario](/wiki/Super_Mario "Super Mario")* franchise. Players control toy [radio-controlled cars](/wiki/Radio-controlled_car "Radio-controlled car") which race around the player's home, streaming video from the onboard camera in the karts into the video game.[[2]](#cite_note-TheVerge-2) It received favorable reviews and sold 1.73 million units worldwide as of December 31, 2022[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Mario_Kart_Live:_Home_Circuit&action=edit).

## Gameplay

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mario_Kart_Live:_Home_Circuit&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Gameplay")]

Players build racing playsets around the house. The radio controlled cars are equipped with video cameras,[[3]](#cite_note-IGN-3) controlled via the Nintendo Switch.[[4]](#cite_note-Engadget-4) The track has many traditional *Mario Kart* items.[[5]](#cite_note-ComicBook-5) The game supports up to 4 players,[[6]](#cite_note-GamesRadar-6) either human or [AI](/wiki/Artificial_intelligence "Artificial intelligence").[[7]](#cite_note-Siliconera-7) The [Grand Prix](/wiki/Grand_Prix_motor_racing "Grand Prix motor racing") mode has the player race against [Bowser Jr.](/wiki/Bowser_Jr. "Bowser Jr.") and the [Koopalings](/wiki/Koopalings "Koopalings"),[[8]](#cite_note-Gematsu-8) and unlocks new customization options and costumes for the playable characters.[[9]](#cite_note-VG247-9) The level creation in *Home Circuit* is performed by placing guidance arrows along four gates to make checkpoints which advance the player through the five-lap race.[[10]](#cite_note-Shacknews-10)

## Development

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mario_Kart_Live:_Home_Circuit&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Development")]

[Velan Studios](/wiki/Velan_Studios "Velan Studios") developed the [mixed reality](/wiki/Mixed_reality "Mixed reality") prototype for *Home Circuit*, and demonstrated it to [Nintendo](/wiki/Nintendo "Nintendo") staff, who were "thrilled" with "its potential".[[11]](#cite_note-NLife-11) The game was primarily developed by Velan Studios, in collaboration with [Nintendo EPD](/wiki/Nintendo_EPD "Nintendo EPD")'s Production Group No. 4, [Nintendo European Research & Development](/wiki/Nintendo_European_Research_%26_Development "Nintendo European Research & Development") and [Nintendo PTD](/wiki/Nintendo_PTD "Nintendo PTD").[[12]](#cite_note-EurogamerProto-12)

*Home Circuit* was revealed in a [Nintendo Direct](/wiki/Nintendo_Direct "Nintendo Direct") on September 3, 2020, to celebrate the *[Super Mario](/wiki/Super_Mario "Super Mario")* franchise's [35th anniversary](/wiki/Super_Mario_Bros._35th_Anniversary "Super Mario Bros. 35th Anniversary"),[[13]](#cite_note-Polygon-13) where it was scheduled for release on October 16, 2020.[[14]](#cite_note-GameInformer-14)[[15]](#cite_note-NLifeDigital-15) A toy set featuring [Mario](/wiki/Mario "Mario") and another set featuring [Luigi](/wiki/Luigi_(character) "Luigi (character)"), including their respective drivers, four gates, arrow signs, and a charging cable,[[16]](#cite_note-EscapistMagazine-16) were also announced shortly after the Direct,[[17]](#cite_note-Kotaku-17) and pre-orders became available shortly after.[[18]](#cite_note-NLifePreOrders-18)

Velan Studios stated that the controls were designed to be easy to understand, while remaining faithful to the *Mario Kart* series.[[19]](#cite_note-GameSpot-19)[[20]](#cite_note-CNET-20)

In October 2020 [Surrogate.tv](/wiki/SurroRTG "SurroRTG") created an unofficial remote play version using fifteen Nintendo Switches and three sets of four karts, with the game physically located at a custom track in Finland.[[21]](#cite_note-21)[[22]](#cite_note-22)

## Reception

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mario_Kart_Live:_Home_Circuit&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Reception")]

Reception

Aggregate scores

| Aggregator | Score |
| --- | --- |
| [Metacritic](/wiki/Metacritic "Metacritic") | 75/100[[23]](#cite_note-MC-23) |
| [OpenCritic](/wiki/OpenCritic "OpenCritic") | 44% recommended[[24]](#cite_note-OpenCritic-2020-24) |

Review scores

| Publication | Score |
| --- | --- |
| *[4Players](/wiki/4Players "4Players")* | 77/100[[25]](#cite_note-25) |
| *[Game Informer](/wiki/Game_Informer "Game Informer")* | 7.5/10[[26]](#cite_note-26) |
| *[GameSpot](/wiki/GameSpot "GameSpot")* | 7/10[[27]](#cite_note-27) |
| *[IGN](/wiki/IGN "IGN")* | 7/10[[28]](#cite_note-28) |
| *[Jeuxvideo.com](/wiki/Jeuxvideo.com "Jeuxvideo.com")* | 16/20[[29]](#cite_note-29) |
| *[Nintendo Life](/wiki/Nintendo_Life "Nintendo Life")* | 8/10[[30]](#cite_note-30) |
| *[Polygon](/wiki/Polygon_(website) "Polygon (website)")* | Recommends[[31]](#cite_note-31) |

The game received "generally favorable" reviews, according to [review aggregator](/wiki/Review_aggregator "Review aggregator") website [Metacritic](/wiki/Metacritic "Metacritic").[[23]](#cite_note-MC-23) 44% of critics recommended the game, with a top critic average of 74/100, according to [OpenCritic](/wiki/OpenCritic "OpenCritic").[[24]](#cite_note-OpenCritic-2020-24) Within its first week of release in Japan, 73,918 copies were sold, making it the best-selling retail game of the week in the country.[[32]](#cite_note-32) It was nominated for the category of Best Family at [The Game Awards 2020](/wiki/The_Game_Awards_2020 "The Game Awards 2020").[[33]](#cite_note-33) During the [24th Annual D.I.C.E. Awards](/wiki/24th_Annual_D.I.C.E._Awards "24th Annual D.I.C.E. Awards"), the [Academy of Interactive Arts & Sciences](/wiki/Academy_of_Interactive_Arts_%26_Sciences "Academy of Interactive Arts & Sciences") awarded *Mario Kart Live* with "[Racing Game of the Year](/wiki/D.I.C.E._Award_for_Racing_Game_of_the_Year "D.I.C.E. Award for Racing Game of the Year")", along with nominations for "[Immersive Reality Technical Achievement](/wiki/D.I.C.E._Award_for_Immersive_Reality_Technical_Achievement "D.I.C.E. Award for Immersive Reality Technical Achievement")" and "[Outstanding Technical Achievement](/wiki/D.I.C.E._Award_for_Outstanding_Technical_Achievement "D.I.C.E. Award for Outstanding Technical Achievement")".[[34]](#cite_note-34)

As of December 2022, *Mario Kart Live: Home Circuit* has sold 1.73 million units.[[35]](#cite_note-CESA2023-35)
